subject: Mathematics
title: Arcs, Sectors, and Segments
description: A tiny lesson with a big payoff: Arcs, Sectors, and Segments.

hook.text: Cut a pizza into slices. Congratulations—you've just made sectors.

buildup.text: An arc is a piece of the circle's edge. A sector is the pie-slice between two radii.

discovery.text: A sector's area is proportional to its central angle. A 90-degree sector is a quarter of the circle.

twist.text: A segment is the region between a chord and an arc—trickier to calculate than a sector.

climax.text: Radar screens, pie charts, and clock faces are all built from arcs and sectors.

punchline.text: Every pizza slice is a lesson in circle geometry.

quiz.question: What is a sector of a circle?
options: The pie-slice region between two radii
